heading: Sale or exchange of patents
label: 1235 Sale or exchange of patents
notes: Amendments2014—Subsec. (b)(2)(B). , substituted “subsection (c)” for “subsection (d)”.Pub. L. 113–295, § 221(a)(82)(B)Subsecs. (c) to (e). , redesignated subsecs. (d) and (e) as (c) and (d), respectively, and struck out former subsec. (c). Prior to amendment, text of subsec. (c) read as follows: “This section shall be applicable with regard to any amounts received, or payments made, pursuant to a transfer described in subsection (a) in any taxable year to which this subtitle applies, regardless of the taxable year in which such transfer occurred.”Pub. L. 113–295, § 221(a)(82)(A)1998—Subsec. (a). , substituted “18 months” for “1 year” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 105–206, § 6005(d)(4), substituted “1 year” for “18 months” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 105–206, § 5001(a)(5)1984—Subsec. (a). , (e), substituted “6 months” for “1 year”, applicable to property acquired after , and before . See Effective Date of 1984 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 98–369, § 1001(b)(19)June 22, 1984Jan. 1, 1988Subsec. (d). , substituted “section 267(b) or persons described in section 707(b)” for “section 267(b)” and “section 267(b) and (c) and section 707(b)” for “section 267(b) and (c)” in introductory provisions, and substituted “section 267(b) or 707(b)” for “section 267(b)” in par. (1).Pub. L. 98–369, § 174(b)(5)(C)1976—Subsec. (a). , provided that “9 months” would be changed to “1 year”.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1402(b)(2), provided that “6 months” would be changed to “9 months” for taxable years beginning in 1977.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1402(b)(1)(V)1958—Subsec. (d). substituted provisions set out as subsec. (d) for provisions reading “Subsection (a) shall not apply to any sale or exchange between an individual and any other related person (as defined in section 267(b)), except brothers and sisters, whether by the whole or half blood.”Pub. L. 85–866

children.text: payable periodically over a period generally coterminous with the transferee’s use of the patent, or
contingent on the productivity, use, or disposition of the property transferred.
any individual whose efforts created such property, or

children.label: A
children.text: the employer of such creator, nor

children.label: B
children.text: related to such creator (within the meaning of subsection (c)).
the phrase “25 percent or more” shall be substituted for the phrase “more than 50 percent” each place it appears in section 267(b) or 707(b), and
paragraph (4) of section 267(c) shall be treated as providing that the family of an individual shall include only his spouse, ancestors, and lineal descendants.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: d Cross reference
children.text: For special rule relating to nonresident aliens, see section 871(a).
